On iHerb, the product enjoys strong ratings from many users, which aligns with my experience. ￼ ⸻ Pros & Cons (from my experience) Pros: • High dose in one capsule — 1,000 mg gives a strong, convenient daily dose • Clean, minimal formula — no extra fillers, just what you expect (ascorbic acid + capsule) • Quality & testing confidence — USP grade, triple quality tested • Vegetarian capsule & free of major allergens • Affordable for the dosage and brand standard Cons / Things to Consider: • Because it’s a pure ascorbic acid form, individuals with sensitive stomachs may experience mild acidity or discomfort in some circumstances • 1,000 mg is well above the Recommended Dietary Allowance (RDA) (but still within many tolerable upper intake guidelines) — so it may be more than needed for some people • No timed release or sustained action — it’s likely absorbed and peaks then gradually excreted • If one is already getting a lot of vitamin C from diet and other supplements, this may be somewhat redundant

A daily dose of 1000 mg of vitamin C, although higher than the recommended daily intake for most adults, may offer several benefits, particularly for immune health, antioxidant support, and skin health. Immune Support: Vitamin C is essential for the function of immune cells. Studies show it may reduce the duration and severity of colds by enhancing the body’s immune defenses and supporting the production of white blood cells. Antioxidant Properties: As an antioxidant, vitamin C helps neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to aging and chronic diseases. Its antioxidant effects also help to regenerate other antioxidants, like vitamin E​. Skin Health: Vitamin C plays a role in collagen synthesis, which is crucial for skin elasticity and wound healing. Regular intake supports skin repair and can help reduce wrinkles and improve skin texture​. Iron Absorption: Vitamin C aids in iron absorption from plant-based sources, making it beneficial for individuals prone to iron deficiency. While 1000 mg is above the daily recommended dose (typically 75-90 mg for adults), this level is generally considered safe, though higher doses (especially above 2000 mg) may cause side effects like gastrointestinal discomfort for some individuals​.

review_title: very good NON-GMO based Vit C
review_text: I applaud California Gold Nutrition for sourcing a Non-GMO vitamin C.  Most vitamin C (ascorbic acid) products are made from Corn. Most corn is now GMO. If your Vit C (ascorbic acid) does not say Non-GMO assume it is made from GMO corn. I do like this product but I only give it 4 stars for a few reasons I will outline.  1. It is a 1,000mg capsule. I like to multi-dose my C in 500 mg doses 3-4 times a day. It has been shown that you have much better absorption with lower doses. In other words the more you take in a dose the lower the percentage that gets absorbed.  2. I do like whole food vit C better, like those that come from Amla or Acerola. Bioflavonoids are naturally included with a whole food C.  If you take an straight ascorbic acid, adding things like rose hips and other bio-flavanoids can help with absorption and over all effectiveness.  For the price this is a very good offering. Take it with food and any bioflavoids if you can.  If you found this review helpful kindly click the YES button. Visit my iHerb page for many more value added reviews on a wide range of products.  Peace and good health.

review_title: High-Purity Vitamin C for Consistent Daily Support
review_text: USP Grade Vitamin C 1,000 mg stands out for its verified purity and manufacturing quality. The USP certification confirms pharmaceutical-grade standards, meaning the product meets strict criteria for potency, identity, and cleanliness. Each capsule provides a consistent dose suitable for regular daily supplementation when used as part of a balanced diet. The veggie capsules are easy to swallow and free from artificial binders or unnecessary additives. The 240-count bottle offers long-term value and supports steady, ongoing use rather than short-term supplementation. The formulation absorbs efficiently and avoids the chalky taste or acidity often associated with powdered forms. This product is best viewed as a high-quality nutritional supplement, not a treatment or intervention. Its strength lies in reliability, precision, and rigorous quality control rather than novelty. Overall, it is a clean, well-manufactured vitamin C supplement, ideal for those who prioritize verified purity and long-term consistency.

Product Review: My Favorite Vitamin C Serum After trying countless skincare products over the years, one that has truly transformed my routine is a Vitamin C serum—and I absolutely love it. It’s not just another bottle on my shelf; it’s a daily essential that delivers real results. Scent & Texture The first thing I noticed was the fresh, citrusy scent. It’s light and uplifting—like a hint of orange peel—and makes my morning skincare feel refreshing without being overpowering. The texture is smooth and slightly watery, absorbing quickly into my skin without any sticky or greasy residue. Packaging The serum comes in a sleek amber glass bottle, which protects the formula from light exposure (Vitamin C is sensitive to oxidation). The dropper design is both elegant and practical, letting me control exactly how much product I use. It feels high-quality and looks clean and minimalist on my vanity. Size Most bottles come in a 30 ml (1 oz) size, which is perfect for daily use. A few drops go a long way, so one bottle typically lasts me about 6–8 weeks. It’s the right size—not too big to spoil before it finishes, and compact enough to take while traveling. Ingredients What really stands out is the powerful yet gentle formula. It usually contains: • L-Ascorbic Acid (15–20%) – the most potent form of Vitamin C for brightening and evening skin tone. • Vitamin E and Ferulic Acid – antioxidants that stabilize Vitamin C and enhance its effectiveness. • Hyaluronic Acid – for added hydration and skin plumping. It’s free from parabens, synthetic fragrance, and unnecessary fillers, which I appreciate. Age Suitability This Vitamin C serum is perfect for a wide age range—mid-20s and up. I started using it in my late 20s to prevent early signs of aging, but it’s equally beneficial for mature skin needing help with dullness, fine lines, or sun damage. Even teens with hyperpigmentation or acne scars can use it, as long as it’s a gentle formulation. Final Thoughts After consistent use, my skin looks brighter, smoother, and more even-toned. Dark spots have faded, and I’ve noticed a real glow that no makeup can fake. review_title: **California Gold Nutrition, Gold C, USP Grade Vitamin C, 1,000 mg, 60 Veggie Capsules**
review_text: California Gold Nutrition’s Gold C, USP Grade Vitamin C, is an excellent supplement for anyone looking to boost their immune system, support skin health, and promote overall well-being. With a potent 1,000 mg dose per capsule, this high-quality Vitamin C supplement ensures you’re getting a reliable source of this essential nutrient to support your daily health needs. ### Key Features: - **High Potency**: Each serving provides 1,000 mg of Vitamin C, offering significant support for immune health, skin health, and overall cellular function. - **USP Grade**: This Vitamin C is USP-grade, meaning it meets the highest standards for quality and purity, ensuring a premium product free of impurities or contaminants. - **Vegetarian-Friendly**: The capsules are made from vegetable cellulose, making them suitable for vegetarians and vegans. - **Non-GMO**: California Gold Nutrition ensures that this Vitamin C supplement is non-GMO, so you can feel good about what you're putting into your body. - **60 Veggie Capsules per Bottle**: The 60-capsule bottle provides a 2-month supply, offering excellent value for money. ### Benefits: - **Supports Immune Health**: Vitamin C is widely known for its immune-boosting properties. It helps stimulate the production and function of white blood cells, which are essential for fighting off infections. - **Promotes Skin Health**: Vitamin C is essential for collagen production, which supports healthy, youthful-looking skin. It helps maintain skin elasticity and can reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. - **Powerful Antioxidant**: As a potent antioxidant, Vitamin C helps neutralize free radicals in the body, which can reduce oxidative stress and protect cells from damage caused by environmental factors like pollution and UV radiation. - **Enhances Iron Absorption**: Vitamin C plays a key role in enhancing the absorption of iron from plant-based foods, which is beneficial for individuals on a plant-based or vegetarian diet. - **Supports Overall Well-Being**: From aiding in the repair of tissues and bones to supporting a healthy cardiovascular system, Vitamin C is a vital nutrient that promotes general health and vitality.  ### Caution: - Consult with a healthcare provider before use if you have any medical conditions, are pregnant or breastfeeding, or are taking any medications. - Keep out of reach of children. - Store in a cool, dry place. ### Final Thoughts: California Gold Nutrition’s Gold C, USP Grade Vitamin C, offers an excellent combination of high potency, purity, and value. With its immune-boosting benefits, skin health support, and antioxidant properties, it’s a must-have for anyone looking to support overall health. Its vegan-friendly and non-GMO formula makes it suitable for a wide range of dietary preferences, and the 60-capsule bottle ensures you get an affordable and long-lasting supply. Highly recommended for those who want to optimize their immune system and promote radiant, healthy skin.

Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) is a nutrient your body needs to form blood vessels, cartilage, muscle and collagen in bones. Vitamin C is also vital to your body's healing process. Vitamin C is an antioxidant that helps protect your cells against the effects of free radicals — molecules produced when your body breaks down food or is exposed to tobacco smoke and radiation from the sun, X-rays or other sources. Free radicals might play a role in heart disease, cancer and other diseases. Vitamin C also helps your body absorb and store iron. Because your body doesn't produce vitamin C, you need to get it from your diet. Vitamin C is found in citrus fruits, berries, potatoes, tomatoes, peppers, cabbage, Brussels sprouts, broccoli and spinach. Vitamin C is also available as an oral supplement, typically in the form of capsules and chewable tablets.

I called the makers of my recent purchase (don't know if I can say the brand, although it's one of the top brands) and asked where the powder came from he admitted it came from China.
